major: Slavic Languages and Literatures, Lower Division B.A.

sections.section_title: GROUP I: LANGUAGE SKILLS FOR RUSSIAN EMPHASIS

notes: MAJOR TRACK IN RUSSIAN LANGUAGE AND LITERATURE This major track integrates the study of the Russian language, Russian literature, and the cultural context of language and literature. If the equivalent of Slavic 45 and 46 are unavailable at a student's community college, these lower-division literature prerequisites are completed at Berkeley upon admission. Language Prerequisites RUSSIAN 1 Elementary Russian (5) NOTE : RUSSIAN 1 formerly SLAVIC 1 RUSSIAN 2 Elementary Russian (5) NOTE : RUSSIAN 2 formerly SLAVIC 2 RUSSIAN 3 Intermediate Russian (5) NOTE : RUSSIAN 3 formerly SLAVIC 3 RUSSIAN 4 Intermediate Russian (5) NOTE : RUSSIAN 4 formerly SLAVIC 4 Literature Prerequisites SLAVIC 45 Nineteenth-Century (3) Russian Literature SLAVIC 46 Twentieth-Century (3) Russian Literature MAJOR TRACK IN RUSSIAN/EAST EUROPEAN/EURASIAN CULTURES The major track in Russian/East European/Eurasian Cultures integrates the study of languages and cultures of a large area: Russia, Eastern/Central Europe and Eurasia. While all majors will be exposed to the knowledge of the whole area, this track allows each student to focus either on one specific cultural region or to combine different regions in their studies (e.g. Russian culture, the cultures of Russia and Caucasus; Balkan cultures; Central European cultures). Each student's program of study must be designed in consultation with and approved by the Major Adviser and will include a language emphasis in one language area as designated below. As East European and Eurasian languages are not offered at California community colleges, the elementary first-year language sequences are started during the student's first fall semester at Berkeley: Polish 25A-B, Czech 26A-B, and BCS 27A-B, Hungarian 1A-1B or Armenian 1A and 1B. For students emphasizing Russian language for this track, if your community college offers Russian 1 and 2 (elementary Russian), plan to take it in preparation for transfer; if it is unavailable, you will still be able to complete Russian 1, 2, 3, and 4 once admitted. In summary, the required 4-semester language sequences can be completed in the two years following admission. Once admitted, students who have a prior background in their proposed target language (Armenian, BCS, Czech, Hungarian, Polish or Russian) will be required to take a language proficiency test or to consult with the relevant department language program coordinator for placement in the correct language course level and credit toward relevant lower-division language courses will be applied to the specific set of major requirements. Introduction to Russian, East European and Eurasian Cultures (SLAVIC 50), which is a prerequisite for this major and is not offered at community colleges, is taken upon admission to Berkeley.

COLLEGE ADMISSIONS REQUIREMENTS FOR TRANSFER STUDENTS THIS MAJOR IS OFFERED BY THE COLLEGE OF LETTERS AND SCIENCE (L&S) By the end of the spring term preceding fall enrollment at Berkeley, you must complete: 1) The L&S Essential Skills Requirements: Reading & Composition, Quantitative Reasoning, and Foreign Language; OR 2) IGETC IMPORTANT information for students applying for admission to L&S for the fall 2025 application cycle and beyond Completion of Essential Skills requirements (R&C, QR, and FL) or IGETC will no longer be a requirement for admission; however, completion of the Essential Skills requirements is strongly recommended prior to enrollment at UC Berkeley. For more information on minimum UC eligibility requirements for transfer applicants for fall 2025 and beyond, please visit the UC Berkeley transfer requirements page . Major Requirements: See details on preparation for this major below. Primary selection criteria for admission, in general: - completion of L&S Requirements (or IGETC), plus - strength of academic preparation, and - grade point average. For more information on admission to UC Berkeley: http://admissions.berkeley.edu For more information on majors at UC Berkeley: Berkeley Academic Guide: http://guide.berkeley.edu

PREPARATION FOR TRANSFER AT THE JUNIOR LEVEL As East European and Eurasian languages are not offered at California community colleges, the elementary first-year language sequences are started during the student's first Fall semester at Berkeley for the following: Polish 25A-B (Introductory Polish), Czech 26A-B (Introductory Czech), Bosnian/Croatian/Serbian 27A-B (Introductory BCS), Hungarian 1A-B (Introductory Hungarian), and Armenian 1A-1B (Introductory Armenian). The required 4-semester language sequence is therefore completed in the two years following admission. Once admitted, students who have a prior background in their proposed target language (Armenian, Hungarian, Polish, Czech, or BCS) may be required to take a language proficiency test or to consult with the relevant department language program coordinator for placement in the correct language course level. Lower-division Russian language courses (equivalent of Russian 1, 2, 3 and 4) must be completed prior to transfer for the Russian Language and Literature track; for the Russian Culture emphasis (the track in Russian/East European/Eurasian Cultures), the equivalent of Russian 1 and 2 is preferred, but otherwise Russian 1, 2, 3, and 4 can be taken upon transfer. Once admitted, students who have a prior background in Russian may be required to take a language proficiency test or to consult with the relevant department language program coordinator for placement in the correct language course level. For more information: Undergraduate Student Services Advisor, Slavic Languages and Literatures (510) 642 4661 issaug@berkeley.edu http://slavic.berkeley.edu/
